summary.intent: Investigate and implement a CrmService trait in the service-traits crate for issue CHA-12, following existing service trait patterns with a NoopCrmService stub and wiring into the Services struct.
summary.outcome: CrmService trait with 15 methods (contacts/matters CRUD, interactions list/create, channels list/link/unlink), NoopCrmService stub, and Services struct integration were successfully implemented in crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs, passing all quality checks, with a PR created and linked to Linear.

repo: service-traits crate uses a Value-based (serde_json::Value) pattern for all service traits — params and returns are untyped to support RPC dispatch without domain crate dependencies

Each service trait follows: #[async_trait] trait XxxService: Send + Sync with ServiceResult<Value> returns, a NoopXxxService stub (list ops return json!([]), reads return json!({}), writes return Err('xxx not configured')), and Arc<dyn XxxService> field in the Services struct
Gateway has a 'crm' feature flag already wired for migrations but no API routes yet — CrmService trait is the service-layer abstraction above the existing CrmStore persistence trait
CRM domain types (Contact, ContactChannel, Matter, Interaction) are fully defined in crates/crm/src/types.rs with PII-safe Secret<String> fields and comprehensive enums
Linear API auth uses an env var (LINEAR_API_KEY) that is not automatically propagated to sandbox/subprocess environments — requires explicit node-based HTTP calls

code.finding: All 23 existing service traits + Services struct aggregation live in a single lib.rs file; CrmService was appended following the same pattern
CrmStore is a low-level persistence trait with SqliteCrmStore and MemoryCrmStore impls using CRM-local crate::Result<T> — distinct from the service-layer ServiceResult<Value>

workflow: Linear comments serve as a persistent workpad across agent runs — investigation and workpad comments are posted separately and appended to on rework runs
Template literal escaping in bash is error-prone for large markdown bodies — writing to a temp file and reading from it is more reliable
Agent checks for existing feature branches and open PRs before creating new ones to avoid duplicates on rework runs

friction: LINEAR_API_KEY env var not propagated to subprocess/sandbox environment — required fallback to node-based HTTP calls for Linear API access
Shell template literal escaping too complex for large markdown comment bodies — required writing to /tmp file as workaround
service-traits/src/lib.rs is a large single file (23 traits), requiring multiple paginated reads to understand full structure and find correct insertion points
open_items: Real CrmService implementation (backed by CrmStore) not yet created — will live in gateway or a glue crate behind the existing 'crm' feature flag
No API routes or RPC handlers for CRM exist in the gateway yet — CrmService trait is only the abstraction layer
interactions_get, interactions_update, interactions_delete methods intentionally omitted per issue scope ('list/add only') — can be added later
Feature flag for CrmService in service-traits not yet added (noted as optional in review feedback)
